title: Annular Solar Eclipse
description: An annular solar eclipse occurs when the Moon passes directly in front of the Sun but appears slightly smaller, leaving a bright ring of sunlight. Proper solar viewing protection is mandatory at all times.

title: Total Lunar Eclipse
description: A total lunar eclipse occurs when the Earth’s shadow completely covers the Moon, often turning it a deep red color. Safe to observe with the naked eye and visible from the night side of Earth.

title: Total Solar Eclipse
description: A total solar eclipse occurs when the Moon completely covers the Sun, briefly turning day into twilight within the path of totality. Proper solar filters are required except during totality.

title: Partial Lunar Eclipse
description: During a partial lunar eclipse, only part of the Moon passes into Earth’s shadow. The darkened portion may appear reddish or dimmed. Safe for naked-eye viewing.

title: Annular Solar Eclipse
description: The Moon passes in front of the Sun but does not completely cover it, leaving a bright ring visible along the edges. Solar viewing protection is required at all times.

title: Penumbral Lunar Eclipse
description: A subtle lunar eclipse where the Moon passes through Earth’s penumbral shadow. The shading is often faint and may be difficult to notice without careful observation.

title: Total Solar Eclipse
description: A dramatic total solar eclipse visible along a defined path across Earth. Observers within the path of totality will witness the Sun’s corona becoming visible for several minutes.
